name: Ilya Sutskever
key_contributions: Co-founded OpenAI in 2015, served as Chief Scientist until 2024
Co-authored AlexNet with Alex Krizhevsky and Geoffrey Hinton (2012) — sparked the deep learning revolution
Pioneered sequence-to-sequence learning with neural networks (2014)
Key architect behind the GPT series scaling strategy at OpenAI
Co-founded Safe Superintelligence Inc. (SSI) in 2024 after departing OpenAI
Student of Geoffrey Hinton at the University of Toronto
summary: Ilya Sutskever is one of the most important figures in modern AI. As a student of Geoffrey Hinton at the University of Toronto, he co-created AlexNet (2012), the convolutional neural network that ignited the deep learning revolution by winning the ImageNet competition by a massive margin. He co-founded OpenAI in 2015 and served as its Chief Scientist, playing a central role in the development of the GPT series and the scaling philosophy that led to GPT-3 and GPT-4. His departure from OpenAI in 2024 and co-founding of Safe Superintelligence Inc. (SSI) signaled a shift toward prioritizing AI safety over rapid capability development.
